title: rhododendron rugosum var. kinabaluense
intro: Rhododendron rugosum var. kinabaluense is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae, native to the mountainous regions of Borneo. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting digestion, due to the presence of bioactive compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Rhododendron rugosum var. kinabaluense can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
